text: L-Leucine (C6H13NO2), chemical formula C6H13NO2, molecular weight 131.17, is a proteinogenic amino acid and essential organic molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, chiral monoclinic space group P21 (No. 4), with unit cell a = 14.666 Å, b = 5.324 Å, c = 9.606 Å, β = 94.06°, Z = 4. L-Leucine exhibits intrinsic piezoelectricity that has been experimentally demonstrated through quantitative direct piezoelectric measurements on crystalline films. The crystal structure (CCDC 1206031) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 5.8% and a density of 1.165 g/cm³. The piezoelectric and electromechanical properties were investigated experimentally and computationally by O'Donnell et al. using DFT calculations and direct piezoelectric measurements in IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation (2020). L-Leucine demonstrates a measured piezoelectric d₃₃ coefficient of approximately 1.57 pC/N with a predicted Young's Modulus of 21 GPa, making it a promising biocompatible, biodegradable alternative to toxic inorganic piezoelectrics for flexible energy harvesting and sensing devices.
isostructural_analogues: Other proteinogenic amino acids in P21 space group
experimental_method: Quantitative direct piezoelectric measurements using commercial d33 piezometer (PiezoTest), quasi-static longitudinal measurement on crystalline films between copper electrodes, optical microscopy characterization
computational_method: Density Functional Theory (DFT) using VASP, PBE functional with GGA exchange-correlation, DFPT for piezoelectric tensor calculation, finite differences method for stiffness tensor
shear_qualitative: Computed (shear coefficients d16, d25, d34, d36 predicted)
dft_shear_qualitative: Maximum piezoelectric strain constant d22 ≈ 8.6 pC/N, d23 ≈ 1.5 pC/N
property_ref_title: Piezoelectricity in the Proteinogenic Amino Acid L-Leucine: A Novel Piezoactive Bioelectret
property_ref_journal: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
